# SingleAxis Fabric — dependency license policy (auditable source of truth)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# This file is the policy consumed by scripts/license_check.py and enforced by
# the License Compatibility CI gate (.github/workflows/license.yml).
#
# The gate is FAIL-CLOSED / ALLOWLIST-based: a dependency license passes ONLY
# if it resolves to a license on the ALLOW list below. Anything not explicitly
# allowed — including unrecognised, missing, or "UNKNOWN" licenses — FAILS the
# build, forcing a conscious human review before the dependency can land.
#
# Line format (one directive per line, '#' starts a comment):
#   ALLOW      <SPDX-id>     Permissive license. Passes the gate.
#   ALLOW-LOG  <SPDX-id>     Weak / file-level copyleft. Passes, but is
#                            surfaced in the report as a notable obligation.
#   DENY       <substring>   Case-insensitive substring. Any dependency whose
#                            (normalized) license string contains it HARD-FAILS,
#                            even if some other token would otherwise allow it.
#
# SPDX identifiers are matched after normalization (scripts/license_check.py
# maps tool-specific spellings — e.g. "Apache Software License",
# "BSD License", "MIT License" — onto canonical SPDX ids before matching).
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# --- ALLOW: permissive licenses ---------------------------------------------
ALLOW MIT
ALLOW BSD-2-Clause
ALLOW BSD-3-Clause
ALLOW Apache-2.0
ALLOW ISC
ALLOW Python-2.0
ALLOW PSF-2.0
ALLOW 0BSD
ALLOW Unlicense
ALLOW CC0-1.0
ALLOW BSD-3-Clause-Clear
ALLOW Zlib
ALLOW BlueOak-1.0.0

# --- ALLOW-LOG: weak / file-level copyleft (allowed, but flagged) -----------
# MPL-2.0 is file-level copyleft: only modified MPL files carry the obligation,
# which is compatible with shipping Apache-2.0 software. Allowed, but logged so
# procurement is aware of the per-file source-availability obligation.
ALLOW-LOG MPL-2.0

# --- DENY: copyleft / restrictive / source-available (hard fail) ------------
# Substring matches are intentional: they catch version-suffixed and
# "-or-later"/"-only" SPDX variants (e.g. GPL-3.0-or-later, LGPL-2.1-only) and
# any vendor spelling that embeds the family name.
DENY GPL          # covers GPL-2.0/3.0 AND LGPL-2.1/3.0 (substring) AND AGPL
DENY AGPL
DENY LGPL
DENY SSPL
DENY BSL          # Business Source License (BSL-1.1)
DENY Business Source
DENY EUPL
DENY CDDL
DENY EPL          # Eclipse Public License (weak copyleft, file-level but
                  # patent-termination terms we don't accept by default)
DENY CC-BY-NC
DENY CC-BY-SA
DENY CC-BY-ND
DENY Commons Clause
DENY Elastic-2.0
DENY copyleft
DENY Proprietary
DENY Commercial
